steps.title: Extract the yearly decay factor
steps.content: The variable $x$ represents months, and the exponent is $\frac{x}{12}$, which converts months to years. After one year, $x = 12$, so the exponent becomes $\frac{12}{12} = 1$. This means the value after 1 year is multiplied by the base: $(0.64)^1 = 0.64$.

steps.title: Convert the decay factor to a percentage loss
steps.content: A multiplier of $0.64$ means the equipment retains $64\%$ of its value each year. The amount lost is $100\% - 64\% = 36\%$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
